2008-09-01.title: Russia Claims Its Sphere of Influence in the World
2008-09-01.abstract: In a speech, Russian President Dmitri A. Medvedev said he rejected “unipolar” U.S. dominance of world affairs.

2008-09-01.title: A Journalist in Russia Is Shot Dead After Arrest
2008-09-01.abstract: A Russian journalist known for his opposition views was arrested in southern Russia on Sunday then fatally shot in the head, suggesting a possible clampdown on domestic dissent.

2008-09-01.title: Doctor Accused in Plot Is Cleared
2008-09-01.abstract: An Indian-born doctor arrested in Australia in July 2007 and accused of aiding in bungled bombings in central London and the Glasgow airport has been cleared of any wrongdoing.

2008-09-01.title: Butter, Not Russian Guns, Worries Merkel’s District
2008-09-01.abstract: While Germany’s chancellor, Angela Merkel, stands at the center of Europe’s response to the Russia-Georgia conflict, many of her constituents would like her to put domestic concerns first.

2008-09-01.title: Tajikistan Hopes Water Will Power Its Ambitions
2008-09-01.abstract: Water is one of the few resources the mountainous Central Asian country possesses in great abundance.

2008-09-02.title: Half a Million Are Stranded by India Flood
2008-09-02.abstract: Authorities have warned that many victims have gone days without food and that drinkable water is running out in overcrowded camps for the displaced, a week after floods struck northern India.

2008-09-02.title: For Thousands of Refugees From the Conflict in Georgia, the Fear Lingers
2008-09-02.abstract: Three weeks after war erupted between Russia and Georgia, many of the 150,000 who have left their homes have not returned.

2008-09-02.title: Two Georgians Went to War but Never Got to Fight
2008-09-02.abstract: The story of two young and inexperienced Georgians’ decision to join the battle against South Ossetia parallels their young nation’s heady rush into combat while relying on an ill-prepared army.

2008-09-02.title: Colombia: Car Bomb in Cali Kills 4
2008-09-02.abstract: A car bomb exploded early Monday near the Palace of Justice in Cali, Colombia’s third largest city, killing 4 people and wounding as many as 20.

2008-09-02.title: Mexico: Gains Against Drugs Claimed
2008-09-02.abstract: President Felipe Calderón, under pressure to crack down on crime, claimed progress on Monday in the fight against drug cartels during an annual report to Mexico’s Congress.

2008-09-03.title: Russia President Dismisses Georgia’s Leader as a ‘Political Corpse’
2008-09-03.abstract: President Dmitri A. Medvedev of Russia made his comments after the European Union strongly criticized Russia for its military offensive in Georgia.

2008-09-03.title: Hezbollah Shrine to Terrorist Suspect Enthralls Lebanese Children
2008-09-03.abstract: Hezbollah has opened an exhibit in honor of Imad Mugniyah, who is accused of masterminding devastating bombings and hijackings in the 1980s and ’90s.

2008-09-03.title: Georgia Eager to Rebuild Its Defeated Armed Forces
2008-09-03.abstract: Georgia’s leaders hope to train the armed forces as if another war with Russia is almost inevitable.

2008-09-03.title: Bolivia: President Challenged on Vote
2008-09-03.abstract: The National Electoral Court has annulled a decree by President Evo Morales to hold a referendum Dec. 7 on a proposed new constitution.

2008-09-03.title: Russia: Journalist’s Killing Condemned
2008-09-03.abstract: The Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe condemned the killing of an opposition journalist in Ingushetia, a volatile region of Russia near Chechnya.

2008-09-04.title: Indian Carmaker Says Local Politics Stopped Factory
2008-09-04.abstract: The Tata Group said its plans to manufacture the world’s least expensive car were halted when it was caught in the middle of a political dispute in West Bengal State.

2008-09-04.title: Support for 2-State Plan Erodes
2008-09-04.abstract: Prominent mainstream Palestinians are warning that they may pursue a one-state solution based on a long-term fight for equal rights within Israel.

2008-09-04.title: Facing Deadly Fish Virus, Chile Introduces Reforms
2008-09-04.abstract: Facing sharp criticism, Chile has introduced measures to improve the sanitary conditions of its salmon industry and reduce the levels of antibiotics used to treat the fish.

2008-09-04.title: Handshake Defuses a Standoff in Baghdad
2008-09-04.abstract: The intensification and resolution of a conflict between a Sunni citizen patrol and the Iraqi Army offered a vivid illustration of the tension between the two organizations.

2008-09-04.title: Ex-Official Faults Politics for Rendition of Canadian to Syria
2008-09-04.abstract: The former leader of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police blamed political interference in the U.S. for the rendition and subsequent torture of Maher Arar, a Canadian engineer.

2008-09-05.title: U.S. Letter Incites Push to Oust India Leader
2008-09-05.abstract: Indian opposition parties say that a secret letter from the State Department shows that Prime Minister Manmohan Singh lied about a controversial deal on nuclear power.

2008-09-05.title: U.S. Offers Storm Aid to Cuba Only Through Relief Groups
2008-09-05.abstract: The State Department said that it had offered humanitarian aid to Cuban victims of Hurricane Gustav provided it does not go through the government of President Raúl Castro.

2008-09-05.title: Donors’ Aid to Poor Nations Declines, U.N. Reports
2008-09-05.abstract: Aid to poor nations has slumped even as higher food and energy prices and slowing global economic growth have made such assistance more urgent, according to a report released Thursday.

2008-09-05.title: Bhutto Widower With Clouded Past Is Set to Lead
2008-09-05.abstract: Asif Ali Zardari will start his tenure as Pakistan’s president burdened by unproven corruption allegations.

2008-09-05.title: A Firing in Guatemala
2008-09-05.abstract: President Álvaro Colom fired his security chief after the discovery of seven unauthorized listening devices and video cameras in the presidential office and residence.

2008-09-06.title: Without Primaries or Caucuses, Campaign for German Chancellor Begins
2008-09-06.abstract: The battle lines in next year’s parliamentary elections in Germany are being drawn the old-fashioned way, through back-room wheeling and dealing.

2008-09-06.title: From the Cuban Underground, a Punk Rocker’s Protest Reverberates
2008-09-06.abstract: Gorki Luis Águila Carrasco’s off-color, ear-splitting lyrics led the Cuban government to charge him with “social dangerousness.”

2008-09-06.title: U.S. Spied on Maliki, Book Says, Upsetting Iraq
2008-09-06.abstract: The Iraqi government warned that claims about espionage in a book by Bob Woodward could affect negotiations over the continuing American troop presence in the country.

2008-09-06.title: Conflict Tests Ties Between the Georgian and Russian Orthodox Churches
2008-09-06.abstract: The two churches expressed dismay after the supposedly unthinkable prospect of two Orthodox nations at war with each other failed to deter armed conflict in August.

2008-09-06.title: A Little Insult Is All the Rage in Venezuela: ‘Pitiyanqui’
2008-09-06.abstract: President Hugo Chávez’s use of a term that roughly translates as “little Yankee” as a contemptuous synonym for “sellout” or “oligarch” has caught on in Venezuela.

2008-09-07.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 6
2008-09-07.abstract: The attack, which also wounded a lawyer, occurred in the town of Zaranj, on the border with Iran.

2008-09-07.title: Cuba Rejects American Offer of Hurricane Aid
2008-09-07.abstract: The Cuban government rejected an offer of disaster aid from the United States after having accepted aid from Russia and other allies.

2008-09-07.title: Formula One boring?!?!?
2008-09-07.abstract: As I predicted some time ago, all it took was for Formula One to have one race with very little action – the European Grand Prix in Valencia two weeks ago – for all sorts of people to start talking about Formula One being boring. It arose this weekend again as Lewis Hamilton’s words were apparently being twisted to indicate that after Valencia he said F1 was boring. He denied that this weekend, on Saturday, in fact.

2008-09-07.title: Prime Minister Chances Early Election in Canada
2008-09-07.abstract: Prime Minister Stephen Harper’s opponents characterized the early vote as political manipulation.

2008-09-07.title: Evidence Points to Civilian Toll in Afghan Raid
2008-09-07.abstract: The Afghan government, human rights officials, and a U.N. investigation back up the account of villagers who say 90 civilians were killed in a U.S. airstrike.

2008-09-08.title: 32 Dead Found, and More Feared, in Cairo Rockslide
2008-09-08.abstract: Authorities were criticized for what many described as a slow government response to the cliff collapse, which some say may have buried at least 100 people.

2008-09-08.title: Hurricane Ike Smashes West Through Caribbean
2008-09-08.abstract: The new storm came on the heels of Gustav, raising the death toll and destruction across the waterlogged region.

2008-09-08.title: On Economy, Voters Move to the Left in Hong Kong
2008-09-08.abstract: Chinese national pride after the Olympics and growing distress over inflation combined to produce a leftward shift in Hong Kong legislative elections on Sunday.

2008-09-08.title: Venezuela Announces Early Plans for Military Exercises With Russia in Caribbean
2008-09-08.abstract: Venezuela has gone out of its way to strengthen relations with Russia, welcoming Russian investment and emerging as a major buyer of Russian arms.

2008-09-08.title: German Party Sees Shake-Up as Its Leader Steps Down
2008-09-08.abstract: Kurt Beck, leader of the Social Democrats, stepped down and Foreign Minister Frank-Walter Steinmeier was chosen to challenge Chancellor Angela Merkel in federal elections next year.

2008-09-09.title: More Afghans Being Killed, Report Says
2008-09-09.abstract: A report by Human Rights Watch said that civilian deaths caused by United States and NATO airstrikes in Afghanistan nearly tripled last year from 2006.

2008-09-09.title: Malaysians Go to Taiwan Amid Strife
2008-09-09.abstract: The Malaysian ruling party’s trip was widely interpreted as a desperate effort to try to stop lawmakers from shifting their support to a prominent opposition figure.

2008-09-09.title: Court to Rule on Thai Leader’s TV Role
2008-09-09.abstract: A conviction for appearing on a television cooking show could force Prime Minister Samak Sundaravej to step down.

2008-09-09.title: T. Norbu, Dalai Lama’s Brother, Dies at 86
2008-09-09.abstract: Thubten J. Norbu was the eldest brother of the Dalai Lama and was also regarded as a reincarnated saint.

2008-09-09.title: South Africa: Furor Over Political Cartoon
2008-09-09.abstract: A cartoon depicting the president of the African National Congress about to rape Lady Justice was condemned by the A.N.C., the party’s youth league and the South African Communist Party.

2008-09-10.title: Iraqi Parliament Returns to Tackle Issue of Election Law
2008-09-10.abstract: The law, stalled by bitter disputes in the last session, is seen as a vital step toward reintegrating Iraqi groups that had been underrepresented in the political process.

2008-09-10.title: Wary of Protests, Russia Puts Few Limits on Smoking
2008-09-10.abstract: Even as it tries to forestall a sharp drop in the population with campaigns that promote family life and a higher birthrate, Russia has barely invested in anti-tobacco ads and education.

2008-09-10.title: Moscow Will Keep Troops in Georgia Enclaves
2008-09-10.abstract: While it agreed to honor a peace deal requiring the withdrawal of its troops from Georgia by October, Russia said troops in breakaway enclaves were not covered by the agreement.

2008-09-10.title: Russia’s Recognition of Georgian Areas Raises Hopes of Its Own Separatists
2008-09-10.abstract: Separatist movements in Russia, all but repressed in recent years, may find new thrust from Russia’s move to recognize the independence of Abkhazia and South Ossetia.

2008-09-10.title: Terror Attack Victims Speak at Forum
2008-09-10.abstract: A United Nations forum sought to to highlight the human toll that organizers said got lost amid most counterterrorism measures.

2008-09-11.title: Amid Growing Unrest, Bolivia Orders U.S. Ambassador to Leave
2008-09-11.abstract: Bolivia said U.S. Ambassador Philip S. Goldberg supported rebellious groups in eastern regions that have been rocked by intensifying protests this week.

2008-09-11.title: A Book Peels Back Some Layers of a Cold War Mystery
2008-09-11.abstract: Thirty years after the death of a Bulgarian dissident writer, injected, it seems, with a poison pellet delivered by a specially adapted umbrella, a book says it makes new revelations.

2008-09-11.title: China: Quake in May Strained Other Fault Systems
2008-09-11.abstract: Earthquake experts have found that the 7.9 magnitude earthquake in Sichuan Province in May added substantial stress to three other major fault systems.

2008-09-11.title: Venezuela: Russian Bombers Arrive for an Exercise
2008-09-11.abstract: Two Russian Tupolev bombers arrived in Venezuela for training exercises with the Venezuelan military.

2008-09-11.title: The Netherlands: Srebrenica Lawsuit Dismissed
2008-09-11.abstract: A district court in The Hague on Wednesday turned down a compensation claim by two families who lost relatives in the 1995 massacre of Bosnian Muslims at Srebrenica by Bosnian Serbs.

2008-09-12.title: Would-Be Protesters Find the Olympics Failed to Expand Free Speech in Beijing
2008-09-12.abstract: People who were kept from protesting are skeptical that there has been any real increase in freedom of speech, despite the hopes of the International Olympic Committee.

2008-09-12.abstract: Prime Minister Vladimir Putin expressed bafflement that those in the West did not accept Russia’s explanation that it acted in self-defense in its conflict with Georgia.

2008-09-12.abstract: The residents of Palomares would like to put a nuclear accident behind them, but the past continues to resurface in the form of radioactive snails and questions about contaminated land.

2008-09-12.title: A West Bank Ruin, Reborn as a Peace Beacon
2008-09-12.abstract: A quiet revolution is stirring in Jenin, once a byword for the extremes of violence between Israelis and Palestinians.

2008-09-12.title: Power of the People Fights Democracy in Thai Protests
2008-09-12.abstract: While they seem like a “people power” revolution, protests in Thailand are more like a counterrevolution by the establishment against the rising electoral power of the mostly rural poor.

2008-09-13.title: 31 Killed in Car Bomb Attack on Iraq Shiite Enclave
2008-09-13.abstract: Sixty people were also wounded in a bombing that occurred at dusk in the predominantly Shiite town of Dujail as many residents prepared to break the Ramadan fast.

2008-09-13.title: In Tangle of Young Lips, a Sex Rebellion in Chile
2008-09-13.abstract: In a country of very traditional social mores, youths are living in an unprecedented period of sexual exploration.

2008-09-13.title: Uzbekistan: Journalist in Drug Trial
2008-09-13.abstract: An Uzbek journalist and human rights advocate, Solizhon Abdurakhmanov, went on trial on charges of trying to sell drugs.

2008-09-13.title: Albania: Whistle-Blower Found Dead
2008-09-13.abstract: A whistle-blower who uncovered evidence of public corruption and deceptive practices by a contractor in a Pentagon-sponsored arms deal was found dead on a mountain road on Friday.

2008-09-13.title: English Channel Tunnel Reopens, Though Travel Chaos Continues
2008-09-13.abstract: Limited passenger and freight train service through the undersea tunnel linking Britain and the Continent resumed after a roaring blaze.

2008-09-14.title: Explosions at 5 Sites in India’s Capital Kill 18
2008-09-14.abstract: The bombings were the latest in a series of terrorist attacks in cities across the country.

2008-09-14.title: Ron Rivera, Potter Devoted to Clean Water, Dies at 60
2008-09-14.abstract: Mr. Rivera pioneered ceramic water filters that cut in half the incidence of diarrhea, a leading cause of death in the third world, especially among children.

2008-09-14.title: Dozens Die in Russian Plane Crash
2008-09-14.abstract: An Aeroflot jet crashed while trying to land in Perm, in central Russia, killing 88 people.

2008-09-14.title: Petraeus’s Final Letter to the Troops
2008-09-14.abstract: Michael R. Gordon, the chief military correspondent for The New York Times, reports that General David Petraeus has drafted a final letter of thanks for the troops ­in one of his last acts as the top American commander in Iraq.

2008-09-14.title: A perfect day at Monza
2008-09-14.abstract: Over breakfast with a fellow journalist this morning we both said to each other how we were thinking that today’s race could be nothing other than extraordinary. With Kimi Raikkonen and Lewis Hamilton starting in 14th and 15th positions and Felipe Massa starting sixth, we knew that whether it rained or not the race would promise to be riveting. The championship leaders would all fight to rise to the surface on a dry track, and who knew what could happen on a wet track?

2008-09-15.title: Afghanistan Is in Its Worst Shape Since 2001, European Diplomat Says
2008-09-15.abstract: Rising food and fuel costs, deteriorating security and the lack of an international response are creating an increasingly precarious situation, a Western envoy said.

2008-09-15.title: Trial Over Cash-Stuffed Suitcase Offers Insight Into Chávez Government
2008-09-15.abstract: At a time when U.S.-Venezuelan relations have reached one of its lowest points in years, a trial about a suitcase stuffed with $800,000 is adding fuel to the fire.

2008-09-15.title: A Climb to Conquer Two Obstacles
2008-09-15.abstract: A trek to the top of Mount Kenya fosters camaraderie among people who have known the painful costs of ethnic conflict.

2008-09-15.title: Two Die After Paris Tourist Boat Sinks
2008-09-15.abstract: The police were investigating whether a small tourist boat that sank Saturday night in the Seine had collided with a bridge or with a larger tourist dinner boat.

2008-09-15.title: Engine Failure Studied in Russian Air Crash
2008-09-15.abstract: Investigators are focusing on engine failure as a possible cause in the crash of an Aeroflot flight in central Russia on Sunday that killed all 88 passengers and crew members on board.

2008-09-16.title: At Chocolate Factory Site, a New Kind of Luxury Box
2008-09-16.abstract: In the capital of the former workers’ state, the first old factory building to be converted into apartments is an icon of Russia’s transition to capitalism.

2008-09-16.title: Georgia Offers Fresh Evidence on War’s Start
2008-09-16.abstract: Georgia says intercepted phone calls show it was under invasion by Russia.

2008-09-16.abstract: The NATO charter stipulates that potential members must resolve outstanding border issues before joining, which could block Georgia’s aspirations.

2008-09-16.abstract: Georgia’s eavesdropping operation was made possible because many South Ossetians used MagtiCom, a United States-owned Georgian mobile operator.

2008-09-16.abstract: Two months after a protester beheaded the wax likeness of Hitler on the opening day of the Madame Tussauds wax museum in Berlin, the figure has been repaired and put back on display.

2008-09-17.title: Populist Appeals in Election, and Claims of Political Theater
2008-09-17.abstract: According to some political analysts, Japan’s Liberal Democratic Party is leery of the real, disruptive change that powerful populists can bring.

2008-09-17.title: Baby Formula Sickened Many More, China Says
2008-09-17.abstract: The new scandal is especially charged politically because government officials pledged to reform food safety regulation after a spate of problems last year.

2008-09-17.title: Arctic Ocean Ice Retreats Less Than Last Year
2008-09-17.abstract: Many Arctic specialists say natural variations in Arctic winds and cloud cover probably had a role in shaping the particularly large ice losses in the past two summers.

2008-09-17.abstract: The governor, Leopoldo Fernández of Pando, was arrested amid growing concern in Washington over the safety of American citizens in Bolivia.

2008-09-17.abstract: The test did not mean that a missile test was imminent, but it showed that North Korea was defying a U.N. resolution by continuing to develop its Taepodong missile series.

2008-09-18.title: Worried Parents in China Wait for Answers on Tainted Formula
2008-09-18.abstract: China’s health minister reported Wednesday that more than 1,300 infants had been hospitalized after being fed contaminated baby formula.

2008-09-18.abstract: Mr. Kitov gained fame for making sensational discoveries about the ancient people of Thrace and helping scientists develop a sharper picture of the kingdom.

2008-09-18.abstract: In a country where prejudice have long restricted opportunities for the disabled, the blind have fiercely defended their exclusive right to the massage business.

2008-09-18.abstract: The suspects were rounded up throughout the U.S. as part of a crackdown on one of Mexico’s largest drug cartels’ routes and distribution network.

2008-09-18.title: New Attacks on Pipelines in Delta of Nigeria
2008-09-18.abstract: The most prominent of the insurgent groups in the region, known as MEND, has threatened to pursue an “oil war” with continuing attacks.

2008-09-19.title: Zimbabwe Power-Sharing Deal Deadlocks
2008-09-19.abstract: The effort to divide the ministries between President Robert Mugabe and the opposition leader Morgan Tsvangirai had been “laborious,” said a negotiator.

2008-09-19.title: D. Dagueneau, Winemaker, Dies at 52
2008-09-19.abstract: Didier Dagueneau was an iconoclastic Loire Valley winemaker whose Pouilly-Fumés displayed a purity and subtlety far beyond most other sauvignon blanc wines.

2008-09-19.title: Saudi Women Find an Unlikely Role Model: Oprah
2008-09-19.abstract: Many women in Saudi Arabia feel a bond with Oprah Winfrey, who speaks about topics rarely aired there.

2008-09-19.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates made his comments as Russia’s president struck a conciliatory tone about improving relations between Russia and the U.S.

2008-09-19.title: War and Drought Threaten Afghan Food Supply
2008-09-19.abstract: More than a quarter of the population may face a harsh winter as a result of a pitiable harvest this year.

2008-09-20.title: In Cairo, Living in Fear at a Cliff’s Sharp Edge
2008-09-20.abstract: The true scope of what happened when a huge piece of a cliff broke off and crushed the lives below remains hidden beneath massive rocks.

2008-09-20.title: Shaky Economy Suddenly Dims Russian Prospects
2008-09-20.abstract: The Russia that has shown confidence under Vladimir V. Putin — a success anchored in stable politics and predictable economic growth — now looks volatile.

2008-09-20.title: Speculation on North Korean Leader Thrives in Factual Vacuum
2008-09-20.abstract: Rumors about Kim Jong-il’s condition run rampant, but analysts say it appears to be business as usual in North Korea.

2008-09-20.title: France Worries About Financial Shock Wave From Across Atlantic
2008-09-20.abstract: The global financial upheaval only makes President Nicolas Sarkozy’s economic lot more complicated.

2008-09-20.title: Indian Police Kill 2 Suspects in Bombings of New Delhi
2008-09-20.abstract: Police identified the mastermind only as Atif, who they said belonged to Indian Mujahedeen, a group that claimed responsibility for the blasts.

2008-09-21.title: Bombing at Hotel in Pakistan Kills at Least 53
2008-09-21.abstract: A huge truck bombing at Islamabad’s Marriott Hotel was one of the worst acts of terrorism in Pakistan’s history.

2008-09-21.title: Olmert Quits Post, and Political Maneuvering Begins
2008-09-21.abstract: The woman who hopes to replace Ehud Olmert, Tzipi Livni, faces formidable obstacles in preserving a coalition.

2008-09-21.title: More Bodies Pulled From Hotel Rubble in Pakistan
2008-09-21.abstract: The death toll in Saturday’s blast outside the Marriott Hotel in Islamabad rose to 53, including two Americans.

2008-09-22.title: In Italy, a Redesign of Nature to Clean It
2008-09-22.abstract: A new breed of landscape architects is recommending a radical solution: not so much to restore environments as to redesign them.

2008-09-22.title: A Spoof Stars a Bumbling Spy, but Many Russian Moviegoers Aren’t Laughing
2008-09-22.abstract: Some have formally called for Russia’s Ministry of Culture to prevent “Hitler Kaput!”- a satirical tale of a Soviet agent — from opening in theaters.

2008-09-22.title: A South African of Charisma and Mystery
2008-09-22.abstract: Jacob Gedleyihlekisa Zuma is posed to succeed Thabo Mbeki as the nation’s leader, but to many, his ideological underpinnings, if they exist at all, have remained opaque.

2008-09-22.title: Land of Gandhi Asserts Itself as Global Military Power
2008-09-22.abstract: In recent years, while world attention has focused on China’s military, India has begun to refashion itself as an armed power with global reach.

2008-09-22.abstract: The deadly truck bomb attack complicates efforts by the new president, Asif Ali Zardari, to fight militants in the tribal areas and cooperate with the United States.

2008-09-23.abstract: To a handful of businessmen, Mount Nyiragongo is central to an effort to transform an area best known as the epicenter of the deadliest conflict since World War II into a tourist attraction.

2008-09-23.abstract: A full-scale battle in a remote corner of Pakistan is shaping up to be a critical test of the military’s determination to combat militants, officials say.

2008-09-23.abstract: President Bush’s top aides are conducting four major new reviews of the war strategy and mission in Afghanistan.

2008-09-23.title: Germany: Baby Polar Bear’s Feeder Dies
2008-09-23.abstract: A zookeeper who achieved unlikely stardom as a surrogate parent for Knut the polar bear was found dead in a Berlin apartment.

2008-09-23.title: Risks for Zimbabwe Deal in Mbeki’s Resignation
2008-09-23.abstract: Thabo Mbeki’s resignation as president of South Africa complicates the power-sharing deal he brokered in Zimbabwe.

2008-09-24.title: Collider Operations on Hold Until Next Year
2008-09-24.abstract: The world’s newest and largest particle accelerator, the Large Hadron Collider, will not begin operations again until April, officials at CERN said.

2008-09-24.title: Terrorism’s Impact Grows as Indian Election Nears
2008-09-24.abstract: India’s fight against terrorism is complicated by a political landscape in which parties vie for Hindu and Muslim voters’ loyalty.

2008-09-24.title: Russia Won’t Meet With U.S. on Iranian Nuclear Program
2008-09-24.abstract: The move is the most significant indication yet of how Russia’s war with Georgia has spoiled relations regarding other security issues.

2008-09-24.title: Tense Rivalries Threaten a Melting-Pot District
2008-09-24.abstract: The 19th Arrondissement is one of the most fascinating districts of Paris — one of the largest, youngest, poorest, most racially diverse — and the most criminal.

2008-09-24.title: Iraqi Trade Officials Ousted in Corruption Sweep
2008-09-24.abstract: At least one of the departing ministry officials and some members of Parliament said the actions were just window dressing intended to shield higher-ups from scrutiny.

2008-09-25.title: Ambush Kills 20 Iraqi Men Searching for Weapons
2008-09-25.abstract: The men were searching for weapons in an agricultural area that had been held by extremist Sunni insurgent groups, the Iraqi police and Army officials said.

2008-09-25.title: Grenade Attack in Mexico Breaks From Deadly Script
2008-09-25.abstract: With nobody to claim responsibility, the authorities consider the brazen attack to be a sign that all bets in the drug war may be off.

2008-09-25.title: Wrestlers Wrest Heritage From Wars’ Wreckage
2008-09-25.abstract: Like the story of Chechnya’s long and often bitter relationship with Russia, the history of Chechen wrestling is filled with frustration, betrayal and loss.

2008-09-25.title: At Canada Terror Trial, the Accused Take on a Less Sinister Cast
2008-09-25.abstract: Many agree that the Toronto 18 may be more a gang that could not shoot straight than Canada’s first serious homegrown terrorist threat.

2008-09-25.abstract: New disclosures have given fresh ammunition to critics who contend that Saddam Hussein was railroaded to the gallows by vengeful officials in Iraq’s new government.

2008-09-26.title: Honor a Literary Giant, but Be Careful Where
2008-09-26.abstract: It was perhaps inevitable that the seemingly simple act of naming a Moscow street in honor of Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn would become complicated.

2008-09-26.title: Iran’s Leader Criticizes U.S. Policies Around World
2008-09-26.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad said that growing world problems like war and poverty are the result of American mismanagement of global affairs.

2008-09-26.title: Radical Settlers Take On Israel
2008-09-26.abstract: A bombing may be the latest sign that elements of Israel’s settler movement are resorting to extremist tactics.

2008-09-26.title: U.N. Receives New Pledges of Aid Totaling $16 Billion
2008-09-26.abstract: Ban Ki-moon, the United Nations secretary general, called it an important signal that the world financial crisis would not impair aid efforts.

2008-09-26.title: Georgia: Bomb Strikes Abkhaz Capital
2008-09-26.abstract: A car bomb exploded in front of a law enforcement building early Thursday morning in the capital of Abkhazia, the breakaway Georgian enclave.

2008-09-27.title: Pakistan’s Faith in Its New Leader Is Shaken
2008-09-27.abstract: Pakistan is struggling with a financial meltdown and terrorism that has hurt confidence in the government.

2008-09-27.title: Russia Flexes Muscles in Oil Deal With Chávez
2008-09-27.abstract: Russia signed a $1 billion military loan to Venezuela and announced plans to modernize its nuclear deterrence.

2008-09-27.title: For a Fee, a Thai Temple Offers a Head Start on Rebirth
2008-09-27.abstract: Buddhism in Thailand can take unexpected forms, embracing animist superstition, magical practices — and the entrepreneurial spirit of many senior monks.

2008-09-27.title: Arab TV Tests Societies’ Limits With Depictions of Sex and Equality
2008-09-27.abstract: While most Arab television dramas are produced in Syria and Egypt, the Arab world’s biggest TV market, in Saudi Arabia, is by far the most culturally conservative.

2008-09-27.title: In Bolivia, a Croat and a Critic Is Cast in a Harsh Light
2008-09-27.abstract: Branko Marinkovic, the scion of a cooking oil and cattle ranching empire, is understandably displeased at being associated with Nazis who fled to South America.

2008-09-28.title: Corrections: For the Record
2008-09-28.abstract: An article last Sunday about President Thabo Mbeki of South Africa, who was pressured to step down in a power struggle with his rival, Jacob Zuma, misspelled, in some editions, the brand of suit that Mr. Mbeki had come to favor during his political career. It is Savile Row, not Seville Row.

2008-09-28.title: Two cases of F1 justice done
2008-09-28.abstract: Fernando Alonso on Saturday threw a fit after his car broke down in qualifying. After setting the fastest time in practice he had good reason to think he would be able to do well in qualifying, then the car broke down. I’d never seen him so demonstratively angry. On race day, it all came together and Alonso showed why he is so good, taking advantage of everything, he and his Renault team pulled off a nearly impossible to imagine victory. It was justice of a kind.

2008-09-28.title: Astronauts Return Safely to China
2008-09-28.abstract: Three Chinese astronauts returned safely to earth in their space capsule after spending nearly three days in low earth orbit and completing the nation’s first spacewalk.

2008-09-28.title: Taliban Claim Responsibility in Killing of Key Female Afghan Officer
2008-09-28.abstract: The police officer, Malalai Kakar, was an iconic figure among women’s groups in Afghanistan and abroad.

2008-09-28.title: U.S. Army Deploys Radar System in Israel
2008-09-28.abstract: The system, which will initially be operated by a U.S. crew, will allow early detection of ballistic missiles and enhance Israel’s defense against attacks by Iran.

2008-09-29.title: Merkel’s Allies Slip in State Vote
2008-09-29.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel’s conservative allies in Bavaria suffered heavy losses in a state election, a development that could have far-reaching consequences for federal elections next year.

2008-09-29.title: Attack May Be Tied to Book About Muhammad
2008-09-29.abstract: A London press agreed to publish “The Jewel of Medina” amid fears that it would offend Muslim extremists.

2008-09-29.title: Far-Right, Anti-Immigrant Parties Make Gains in Austrian Elections
2008-09-29.abstract: Anti-Muslim and anti-immigrant sentiments and rising discontent over higher prices benefited the parties, which won nearly a third of the parliamentary elections vote on Sunday.

2008-09-29.title: Loyalists Lead Vote in Belarus
2008-09-29.abstract: Candidates loyal to authoritarian President Aleksandr G. Lukashenko won an overwhelming majority of parliamentary seats in a closely watched election.

2008-09-29.title: Jews and Muslims Share Holy Season in Jerusalem
2008-09-29.abstract: A calendar coincidence has led to a kind of monotheistic traffic jam along the paths of the Old City.

2008-09-30.title: Details Only Add to Puzzle in Umbrian Murder Case
2008-09-30.abstract: The killing of a British student continues to baffle and intrigue Perugia, Italy, though a year has gone by without any decisive developments.

2008-09-30.title: Vote in Germany Rattles Conservative Bloc
2008-09-30.abstract: The Christian Social Union posted its worst showing in Bavaria since 1954, and Chancellor Angela Merkel’s own Christian Democrats also fared poorly.

2008-09-30.title: Pakistani Refugees Pour Into Afghanistan
2008-09-30.abstract: Fighting in a tribal district near the tense border has driven 20,000 Pakistanis out of the country.

2008-09-30.title: On Exhibit, Georgian War and a New Russian Pride
2008-09-30.abstract: A hastily assembled exhibit at the Russian Central Armed Forces Museum displays Russia’s pride in its victory in the small-bore Georgian conflict.

2008-09-30.title: Accusations Against Leader of Sudan Fuel Debate
2008-09-30.abstract: The possibility that President Omar Hassan al-Bashir might be indicted on charges of genocide and war crimes prompted an intense tug of war at the United Nations General Assembly.
